title: [FIRRTL] Canonicalization missing for narrow dynamic subindex
body:
Input fir file:
```
FIRRTL version 3.0.0
circuit stmts:
  module stmts :
    input reset : UInt<1>
    input clock : Clock
    input vec : UInt<8>[8]
    input index: UInt<2>
    output b : UInt<8> 
    b <= vec[index]
```
output:
```
// Generated by CIRCT unknown git version
module stmts(
  input        reset,
               clock,
  input  [7:0] vec_0,
               vec_1,
               vec_2,
               vec_3,
               vec_4,
               vec_5,
               vec_6,
               vec_7,
  input  [1:0] index,
  output [7:0] b
);

  wire [7:0][7:0] _GEN =
    {{vec_7}, {vec_6}, {vec_5}, {vec_4}, {vec_3}, {vec_2}, {vec_1}, {vec_0}};
  assign b = _GEN[{1'h0, index}];
endmodule
```

Since `index` is 2-bit integer the range is `[0, 4)` so we can drop upper elements (vec_4 ~ vec_7). 
We can perform this canonicalization in HW(hw.array_get) and FIRRTL (firrtl.multibit_mux)
